author: @babagroo3464
text:
Super fire vid brother, especially the goated pulls at the end. 

To answer some of your questions, the way you know who is a good pitcher is mainly by their pitch selection. "Pitch Sequencing" and "Tunneling" (when pitches look the same) are terms to know. As well as speed differential ( 99 fastball and an 83 changeup). So look for pitches that have high speed differential and good pitch combos like sinker/cutter, fastball/changeup, slider/slurve, fastball/splitter, and others. 

You pointed it out with the second wheel spin, but a lot of players in this game are not available in the auction house. Most notably, program capstones and set cards. Those are extremely good cards that you'll want to have access to on the wheel. So be sure to grind out the programs/sets or pay someone to lol. 

One thing on the gameplay is it is an advantage to know what your opponents' pitches are. You can see that by holding RT prior to the pitch being thrown when you're at the plate. 

And then lastly a couple baseball rules: 
-If there is 2 outs and a ball is put in play, a run cannot be scored unless that batter reaches base -- i.e. just get the force out 
-A pitcher can only pitch once in game, you figured that one out haha
-A pitcher must face 3 batters
-extra innings will start with a runner on 2nd
video_title: The Wheel of MLB the Show!

author: @vichasfunlol
text:
Asking for an Among us mod part 7 (I think)

If Ssundee actually sees this comment, I recommend making a mod about the movie "The Black Phone." There are two movies in the series, so the map will be fairly decent in size. For an imposter transportation item, I recommend ice skates (which could also be used to eliminate a player) for the snow area and the black van for the regular area. For crewmates, I recommend the transportation item being Billy Showalter's bike, which can be attained by completing the quest that he gives. 

Here are some suggestions for the quests.

Robin Arelleone, quest- Fight Moose. 
Ability for crewmates- stun other players with a punch. 
Ability for imposter- Eliminate a player with a punch (one time, afterwards only stuns players)

Billy Showalter, quest- Deliver six newspapers
Ability for crewmates- Billy's bike for transportation
Ability for imposter-Billy's bike for transportation and running over players (one time, afterwards only stuns players)

Griffin Stagg, quest- Protect him against a three-wave attack from bullies 
Ability for crewmates- Call Griffin to temporarily protect them for 120 seconds
Ability for imposter- Call Griffin to eliminate a player of your choosing (one time, afterwards only stuns players)

Vance Hopper, quest- Beat him in pinball
Ability for crewmates- Summons a pinball machine that could be used to hide from other players.
Ability for imposter- Summons a pinball machine to crush players (multiple uses with a 100 second cooldown)

Bruce Yamada, quest- Hit a baseball farther than he can
Ability for crewmates- Temporarily has a baseball gold medal that deflects any attacks onto a nearby crewmate.
Ability for imposter- Can turn the gold medal giant and roll it across the map, eliminating the first two players it hits (one time, afterwards only stuns players)

Finney Blake, quest- Help him find his rocket flashlight.
Ability for crewmates- Temporarily see in the dark with a flashlight when lights go out.
Ability for imposter- Disintegrate whoever is in the flashlight ray, first two players it reaches permanently eliminated (one time, afterwards only stuns players)

Felix, quest- Find his remains in the frozen lake in the snow area.
Ability for crewmates- Call Felix's ghost to halt the imposter form moving for thirty seconds.
Ability for imposter- Call Felix's ghost to jump scare the selected player, causing disorientation, hallucinations, and possibly blinding them (multiple uses on a 120 second cooldown)

Cal, quest- Find his remains in the supply shed close to the frozen lake
Ability for crewmates- Gives players an ax to temporarily stun the imposter.
Ability for imposter- Use ax to cut someone's face in half on a tree stump (one time, afterwards only blinds players)

Spike, quest- Find the remains of his jacket in the furnace of the food hall in the snow area.
Ability for crewmates- Summons a jacket that protects them from attacks for thirty seconds (120 second cooldown)
Ability for imposter- Eliminate a player by wrapping them in the jacket, causing a heatstroke (multiple uses, long cooldown)

Their can also be a crewmate role which I recommend should be Gwendolyn (Gwen) Blake, after completing all their tasks, they get a dream that reveals who's the imposter. So it'll lead to a challenge where the imposter has to figure out who is the Gwen role.


For the imposter skin, I recommend it being the Grabber due to the fact he's the main villain in both movies. If Ssundee and his team actually make this a mod for a video, I would deeply appreciate it

author: @yokawa17
text: Nobody has as much passion as vico out of all the pros. It is really important that he won for fortnite competitive because he is someone who shows that he really cares about the game and takes it seriously.That will attract more new players and different kind of players. He is really good for the media and inspiring for upcoming pros to show passion and emotion. Since Kids look up to the best player, it is also nice to have a proper role model now. Alot of the pros are crying when something doesn't work out for them. Many of them say that they are done or want to quit the game every 5th sentence, or say its boring and so on. Of course the reason for that is that fortnite is just a really young esport and thats why the players are not that mature. But maybe vico will inspire more people to show proper sportsmanship and that will make the game bigger. The game becomes harder and crybabies are starting falling behind. This LAN showes that playing the game alot won't be enough in the future. Additionally it becomes more important that you have the will and the passion for it. I'm excited to see how fortnite comp will evolve.
video_title: FNCS MAJOR 1 SUMMIT | FINALS | DAY 3

author: @Tenchigumi
text:
I have no idea how I missed your previous Half-Life video; I swear I didn't get an notification until THIS video popped up. Anyway, marathoning these two should make my weekend OT more bearable. 

Like death and taxes, sequels to profitiable new IPs are something we can't escape from, but we can at least hope that they'll lead to something better. That said, my fingers have been crossed for Half-Life 3 so long that they're basically a caduceus now. 

While I certainly enjoyed Half-Life 2's gameplay, presentation and story when I first played it decades ago, I still remember it more by its technical achievements than the game itself. Gaming magazines like Maximum PC lauded its groundbreaking usage of Direct X9, with one of its examples being their usage of shaders that ensured that water along shorelines appeared seamlessly transparent, rather than the stark polygonal outlines we'd seen in nearly every 3D game up to that point (the latter being a problem that persisted in AAA games as late as Skyrim). I also remember the game running far smoother than World of Warcraft (which released the same year), as I was able to finish HL2 on a somewhat budget PC, but WOW forced me to build a gaming PC. Looking back, HL2 achieved a feat PC games rarely accomplished: presenting something that technically appeared far ahead of its time, but performed admirably on hardware well before its time.

Man, I totally forgot about the wild legal battles between Vivendi and Valve (and probably didn't understand it all too well back then). Considering what I know now about corporate America, having internal memos outright stating orders to destroy records is unfathomably reckless; anyone who's suffered through corporate compliance training knows this. And it's even more baffling that Vivendi thought a mere Korean language barrier would be enough to shield them; considering that they were based in Los Angeles (a region that is home to one of the largest Asian populations outside of Asia itself) they really should have known better. Furthermore, Valve is based in Washington, which has a solid Asian population of its own. Altogether, the odds of a Korean speaker being present between the two companies (or available for legal counsel) was _incredibly_ high. Valve managed to dodge a bullet simply because Vivendi held the gun backwards.
video_title: Masterpiece or Madness? - Half Life 2 & Episodes Retrospective & History

author: @tedraymondLP
text:
I’m happy Arin addressed some of the comments on this series. 

I’ve been having a blast with this run, and it’s been funny and interesting to watch. It may not be a “competent romp,” as Arin said, but that’s what makes it compelling! I hope the boys keep this up even longer and I’m also glad it seems to be doing well, by Arin’s own admission, viewswise. Thanks @GameGrumps! 

Here’s where the Grumplocke stands so far: 
Gym badges: 5
Available Pokémon: 5 in team, 6 in box (+1 Buneary for Amity Square) - Arin is headed to get a Riolu egg. 
Dead Pokémon: 10 
Dan's quiz score: 30%

Arin’s most recent catch was in Ep. 27: Greg the Gastly in Lost Tower. 
Arin’s most recent death was in Ep. 23: Bob the Floatzel to a Graveler’s self-destruct.

He has not caught anything on the following routes: 205, 208, 209*, 210, 212, 213, 214, 215, 218, 219, -220, and 221- (e: hasn't been here yet). He’s also not caught anything in Ravaged Path, Floaroma Meadow, the Old Chateau, the Ruin Maniac’s Cave, the Trophy Garden, nor has he fished in any towns with water. The gift Porygon remains unclaimed in Veilstone City. (*Some nuzlockes consider Lost Tower and Route 209 to be the same location.) Lots of options available! 

I guess we're facing Byron next week! How will Arin handle the Steel-type gym? He's leveled-up and has options, but there could be risk!

To be continued ⬅
video_title: FEAR IS AN ABBARATION | Pokemon Grumplocke [32]

author: @warn_the_blind
text: My only real criticism is that I would have happily watched another hour of him just mapping and exploring the area. That said, I completely understand the filmmakers’ approach. The goal was to make a movie that works for a general audience, many of whom have probably never heard of the Backrooms before, whereas I’m coming in with a clear bias toward that slow paced liminal space niche. I know I’m probably in the minority there. I’ve seen some people say the film started to drag near the end, but I was genuinely ready to spend another hour watching more.

author: @hampter_double0100
text:
About the Day system in this game: The Day symbols are meant to be The Seven Deadly Sins. Each of the core characters of those specific days representing the sins. 

These are:
Bishop > Purple > Pride
Astrologist > Blue > Sloth
Inquisitor > Red > Wrath
Ms. Charm > Pink > Lust
Snake > Green > Envy
The Merchant > Orange > Gluttony 
Now, there are 6 sins and days here. Which leaves Greed. That is you. You, the player represent Greed. 
I certainly think that this game could use some rework at some points, but this little part of the lore is very cool to me.
video_title: I 100%'d Graveyard Keeper. It Was Brutal.

author: @Snickersnek
text:
Fun fact, "Bird type" WAS a thing in Gen 1! While it was replaced with "Flying" type, Missingno is Bird/Normal, the only instance of a true Bird-type pokémon. So at least for a while in development, it would seem that "Bird" was considered a type as much as Bug and Dragon. 
With that in mind, Bird-keepers and the Gym tutor NPC mentioning the bird-type makes a lot more sense.

author: @jingl4060
text:
Here’s a recipe for a cake 

Baked Oreo Cake

Oreo

Ingredients

* 24 Oreo cookies
* 240 ml milk
* 1 tsp baking powder
* Optional:
    * 2 eggs (for a fluffier cake)
    * 1 tsp vanilla extract
    * Extra crushed Oreos for topping

Instructions

1. Preheat the oven
    * 180°C (160°C fan-forced).
2. Make the batter
    * Blend the Oreos into fine crumbs.
    * Add milk and baking powder.
    * If using eggs and vanilla, add them now.
    * Blend until smooth.
3. Prepare the pan
    * Grease an 18–20 cm cake tin or line it with baking paper.
4. Bake
    * Pour the batter into the tin.
    * Sprinkle extra crushed Oreos on top if desired.
    * Bake for 25–30 minutes, or until a skewer inserted in the center comes out mostly clean.
5. Cool
    * Let the cake cool in the tin for 10 minutes.
    * Transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

Optional Oreo Frosting

Mix:

* 250 ml whipped cream
* 4 crushed Oreos

Spread over the cooled cake and decorate with whole Oreos.

text: Day 5 of asking to do my New video idea: Minecraft but every drop is one piece! You, tico, and, izzy can choose to be any member in the one piece crew. Some drops only work for your character that you choose but others all can use. Say for an example if izzy chooses zoro, he can use all of his swords, but is bonc chooses luffy, then he cant use them. You can also learn ability based on your xp level, when it reaches 100 you have your max form (like gear 5) you also get an extra row of hearts if you reach lvl 100. And there is a hidden chest out at sea and if someone can find this they get WAYYYYYY too powerful. And modded structures.

author: @walsh451
text:
Here in the UK the average new game went from £50 to £70 overnight. With everything else going up, wages generally stagnating, you have to consider far more what you spend your money on. I now wait for games to come down in price, for example I really enjoyed the BF6 beta, but was not going to pay full price, I only got it a few weeks ago on sale for £35. I have a list as long as my arm of games I am "waiting on" to come down in price more. I brought FIFA every year on release from 1998-2020, but the increase in Price was far too much. If games were still £50 I would buy more newer titles.

Add to that the fact that allot of games come out with problems that need patching, it only really makes sense to wait it out.

author: @cwsojourner
text: I'm an Old Gamer (played Pong and Space Invaders on Atari as a kid...), and I can tell you for a fact that games used to be released in playable form 99.9% of the time. This was simply because there was no convenient way for updates to reach the consumer. So, I know it's possible. The executives at gaming company, most of whom are from Business School and not Coding School, got f-ing complacent and lazy when the Internet became a thing. While it's true that games have scaled up exponentially in terms of complexity and sheer volume of code, so has the the number of people working on the games and the size of the budgets for the games. I'd buy pre-releases of my favorite titles if I knew they were going to work they way they should the first time. But I don't like being forced to be a Beta tester while paying full price. I have better things to do with my time and money, and I've learned patience. Screw 'em!

author: @carlosaubine9560
text: The whole bit about keeping destiny 2 going is a massive misread of the situation. It's over for D2, and nothing will bring me back along with many others if not a brand new game with a fresh start. The tiger tools are old, the content is stale, and the power meta is too far gone and you cannot change that without massive backlash like sunsetting back in the day. D3 should have been in development from the second Final Shape came out, you need a clean slate and a clean jumping on point for everyone after such a big event. Making more expansions for D2 even if they are fantastic will just keep getting less and less players.

author: @ChannelKit
text:
How was Destiny 2's pricing anything other than awful? We had expansions, season passes, dungeon keys, event cards, premium cosmetics, character boosts, and even transmog was originally designed around monetization. Some armor sets were $20+ per character, and crossover cosmetics pushed prices even higher.

On top of that, Bungie regularly priced Silver packs so they didn't match item costs, meaning you often had to buy the next tier up just to get enough currency. Destiny 2 didn't just have microtransactions. It stacked monetization systems on top of monetization systems.
video_title: Sony, you're making a huge mistake | This Week in Videogames

author: @oisinmcphillips2090
text: I mostly agree with the crashout except for the idea that all of what a D3 would achieve can be done in D2. The game is labouring under tech debt, outdated systems, abandoned content islands, so much junk that a true fresh start would be needed imo. Honestly, that Destiny mobile game had the right idea, don’t make a sequel to the same old story, go back to another time in Destiny’s excellent lore and start there. Hundreds of years in the past (or future) and have a clean separation. An update to D2 wouldn’t bring people back at this point, you’d only get that hardcore coming back. New players need an actual onboarding experience that D2 just cannot and will never be able to offer.
video_title: Sony, you're making a huge mistake | This Week in Videogames

author: @Cordis2Die
text:
I think if Bungie will be able to cut the game size like Helldivers 2 did and return all of the cut content back into the game - it will start getting new players and old ones will return. I'm not sure how hard it is to pull off, but I think it's possible.

The only reason I'm not playing Destiny 2 it's because I don't want to start from the middle of the story, I want to start from the beginning and experience all of it myself.
video_title: Sony, you're making a huge mistake | This Week in Videogames

author: @aidanvanstraaten8251
text: The problem with a double down on D2 is that the tiger engine is an albatross tied around Bungie's neck. The reason people want a D3 is not for better graphics or better gunplay or any real change in the gameplay model. People just want a fresh start that erases all the fomo and maybe a new story that doesn't require hours of byf videos to understand.

author: @Marslo
text:
There is a part of me that understands that we're in the sunset phase of gaming's golden age. 

Cost is too high, risk is too great and consumer interest isn't what it used to be. Too many out source of media are competing for their attention.

Gaming has started the very early journey of making its way to one day becoming "legacy media".

author: @Achilles3602
text: I think a big problem about theoretically doing like a "soft relaunch" of destiny 2, with all the new stuff mentioned, new onboarding, etc. is that even then the game has too much baggage, and more importantly it has too much anger. Sunsetting was an awful idea, but players will (arguably justifiably) never ever let it go, any discussion about the game is forever tainted by it (some refer to the big vaulting circa beyond light, some only refer to seasons rotating out, one is more fixable than the other). The player base is also so divided, Bungie at their peak could not fill the chomping maws of both the PVP and PVE player bases, both of which are loud, demanding and highly entrenched in why they play destiny, a smaller Bungie couldn't manage it and the resulting portions of the player base inevitably left wanting would instantly be mad again, tainting any hope of the game getting a fresh start. There's also no trust in Bungie I feel, why invest your time in something that may or may not be bungled so fantastically again like we know Bungie has in the past?
video_title: Sony, you're making a huge mistake | This Week in Videogames

author: @Golemming
text:
problem with your reasoning is that Bungie should have done changes many many years ago. People all over said to Bungie that "vault is a bad idea", "FOMO is a bad idea".

But Bungie ignored everything and now they will be a shell of their former self, if Sony will not close them altogether

author: @any_austin
text:
Try Rocket Money for FREE or unlock more features with premium at: https://RocketMoney.com/anyaustin

Huge thank you to the team at Warhorse Studios for giving me the time of day. And congrats on their newly announced Lord of the Rings and Kingdom Come games. Here are the people who you saw in the call listed in exactly the way that Tobias listed them in the email he sent me:

——

THE UNIMPORTANT ONES

Tobias Stolz-Zwilling, Communications Director
Thomas Grey, Community Manager

THE IMPORTANT ONES

Joanna Nowak – Historical Consultant
Petr Bouška – Concept Artist (formerly an architect) (glasses)
Filip Štorch – Concept Artist (formerly a painter) (Black shirt with heart)

——

I didn’t make the determination of importance so don’t get mad at me. But seriously I love talking to game devs. It’s always surprising and enlightening.

author: @matthewherndon6488
text:
35:13 The part where he's talking to Sophie's "ghost" gave me such _specific,_ storytelling related chills. The same chills I get when reading H.P. Lovecraft or scrolling the SCP wiki. I mean, Mark didn't even put that much into it in the video, but it still played out like a movie in my head:

A desperate survivor finally finds the closest thing he's had to a real human being since his arrival on Proteus, only to hear, "Fatal error. Out of memory. Vivarium halted."

He tries again, with a question he's needed to ask for days, one only another human could provide a satisfying answer to: "Who am I??"

"Fatal error. Out of memory. Vivarium halted."

"I have more questions!" he screams.

"Fatal error. Out of memory. Vivarium halted."

Manic hyperventilating gives way to calmer gasps. Finally, the survivor places a hand on the bio bed, and breathes, "Goodbye, Sophie."

And one last time, the monotone voice of the NoA node echoes, "Fatal error. Out of memory. Vivarium halted."



Not that I want to deprive the world of his let's plays for another 6-10 years, but good god, can you imagine if this man made a Subnautica movie?
video_title: THE HOT ZONE | Subnautica 2 - Part 7

author: @samuelsupernova894
text: That liminal version of central park really hit close to home for me. When I was a little kid and my father was in the hospital, when we'd visit, they'd take me across the street to central park to distract me from all the medical trauma that was happening. They probably thought it'd soothe me with all the nature and playgrounds and such. Instead, central park became a place associated with loneliness, helplessness, and being lied to. I still can't go there without feeling uncomfortable. Seeing the footage of parasite eve where it's completely dark and covered in snow, the protagonist alone and small, with the promise of crowds forever just out of reach... I'll admit I felt something. Fascinating how an old low-poly game can do that.

author: @sonicthehedgegod
text: Idk if it's been mentioned yet, but a fascinating aspect of the twin towers' inclusion in Driver: Parallel Lines is that only the first half of the game takes place in the seventies. Halfway through the main campaign, there's a timeskip to 2006, where the rest of the game plays out. So not only does it include the twin towers, but it includes a New York without them, all within the same game!

author: @tristanrow4951
text: Your discussion of Max Payne's sky brings to mind the opening line of Neuromancer. "The sky above the port was the color of television, tuned to a dead channel." I read an anniversary edition of the book, where in the foreword the author William Gibson comments that today, many readers will no longer have an immediate frame of reference as to what that means. Very fitting.

author: @thomastrinkle2294
text:
I played The Division at launch and for a couple years after. Around the same time I got a new position in the company I worked for and went to the corporate headquarters in Manhattan for training with my new team I would be working remotely with from Texas. 

The first day walking from my hotel to the headquarters building, I froze on the sidewalk because I recognized the corporate art fountain in the plaza outside. I’d had so many firefights around it in The Division.
video_title: all the pixels in new york are freezing

author: @Drunkencrono
text: I have often thought about how lucky NYC is to get all this representation in media thanks to its popularity. Games, music, movies, cartoons, etc. all give a snapshot of a location in such varied, stylized, and real ways. We have so many examples throughout history of not just the way it was but the way the world thinks it was, distributed on a wide scale. There aren't many locations as lucky as that. You don't even have to go far really, what does the average person know about Long Island? What about Rhode Island? What have I ever seen about life in Rancho Cucamonga?

author: @AvsJoe
text: There's something so hypnotic about the style of the overworld Manhattan from Parasite Eve. Like a children's book of New York geography digitally recreated as a city in Final Fantasy VIII. I want to see every major world city redone in this style, from Lagos to Los Angeles - it would be beautiful to compare the world cities in such a strangely-detailed yet simple way.

author: @Omniforgot
text: Parasite Eve's cutscenes fall into the exact perfect level of uncanny that early CGI sometimes achieves. I used to watch the creature transformation scenes all the time as a kid, I don't think anything has ever fully replicated that same uneasy feeling they gave me.

text: "freezing" is an interesting choice of words considering the actual contents of the video.
